title: melaleuca quadrifida subsp. teretifolia
intro: Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. teretifolia is a subspecies of tree native to tropical Northern Australia, known for its distinctive narrow leaves and ability to thrive in harsh environments. This plant has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of compounds such as terpinen-4-ol. The health benefits of Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. teretifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and support the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, essential oil, or salve, which can be used to treat various conditions such as skin irritations and wounds.
